LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF YA-HSIANG HU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA POLICY IS TO LIBERALLY ALLOW SUCH AMENDMENTS. (MESLER V. BRAGG MGMT. CO (1985) 39 CAL.3D 290, 296.) AS NO TRIAL DATE IS SET, PREJUDICE IS MINIMAL. (SEE MORGAN V. SUP. CT. (1959) 172 CAL.APP.2D 527, 530.) LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED FOR FUTILITY ONLY WHEN THE FUTILITY IS PATENT, SUCH AS WHEN A SETTLEMENT OR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS THE CLAIM. (VAILLETTE V. FIREMAN'S FUND INS. CO. (1993) 18 CAL.APP.4TH 680, 685.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/RU

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-15-2020 IN 206, PLAINTIFF YA-HSIANG CHEN HU FOR AN ORDER TO DEEM SERVICE OF DEFENDANT YU-HOW CHEN COMPLETED, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, AN ORDER FOR ALTERNATIVE SERVICE VIA E-MAIL; OR FOR SERVICE VIA WORK ADDRESS. KENNETH HSU, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. BENJAMIN WAGNER, ESQ. AND DERIK RAO, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL PARAGON CORPORATION VIA COURTCALL. MARC PERNICK, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT PAMELA INVESTMENT CORPORATION VIA COURTCALL. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES FROM DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL PARAGON CORPORATION NOR THE JOINDER FROM DEFENDANT PAMELA INVESTMENT CORPORATION BUT WILL HEAR ORAL ARGUMENTS. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO DEEM SERVICE OF DEFENDANT CHEN COMPLETED. THE COURT FURTHER DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO SERVE DEFENDANT CHEN BY EMAIL. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ISSUE IS NOT RIPENED YET AND WILL NOT BE ADDRESSED BY WAY OF THIS ORDER WITH REGARDS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO SERVE DEFENDANT CHEN AT HIS WORK ADDRESS IN CHINA. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AS TO ONE DOCUMENT WHICH IS EXHIBIT E, A GRANT DEED FROM SAN MATEO COUNTY. PLAINTIFF'S REMAINING REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE WITH REGARD TO ALL OTHER EXHIBITS ARE DENIED. DEPARTMENT 206 WILL HEAR THE MOTION TO DISMISS BASED ON CCP 583.240 WITH RESPECT TO THIS PARTICULAR CASE ONLY. MR. WAGNER WILL PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

California policy is to liberally allow such amendments. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) As no trial date is set, prejudice is minimal. (See Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Leave to amend is denied for futility only when the futility is patent, such as when a settlement or statute of limitations bars the claim. (Vaillette v. No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL PARAGON CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Universal Paragon Corporation's demurrer to the first, fourth through seventh and ninth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Ya-Hsiang Chen Hu is overruled as to the fifth cause of action for declaratory relief and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all other causes of action alleged against UPC. Liberally construed, the FAC alleges a dispute warranting a declaration from the court determining who owns the stock in UPC and UPC is an appropriate party to such a declaratory relief claims. All other claims alleged against UPC are confusing about the factual and legal bases for asserted liability against UPC. If he can do so in good faith, plaintiff is given leave to clarify those claims by alleging ultimate facts that support liability against UPC and specifying on what basis (e.g. agency, alter ego, promoter, own misconduct) liability is sought to be established against UPC for each claim.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
